evals.name: setup-reliable-schedule
evals.prompt: I want my backup script at /home/me/backup.sh to run every night at 3am, and I want to actually find out if it ever stops running. Walk me through setting that up so it's reliable.
evals.expected_output: Uses cron_sentinel.py to produce a crontab line that wraps the backup command (wrap --name ... --expect-every 1d), plus a separate watchdog line that runs `check` on a short interval to alert on failures or silence. Explains the two-line pattern and why --expect-every enables silent-failure detection.

assertions.text: Produces a crontab line that runs the backup through cron_sentinel.py wrap
Sets an expected cadence (--expect-every) so overdue/silent runs can be detected
Includes a separate watchdog (check) on its own schedule for alerting

evals.name: detect-silent-failure
evals.prompt: My nightly job hasn't produced output for two days but I never got an error message. Going forward, how do I detect when a scheduled job silently stops running, not just when it crashes?
evals.expected_output: Explains the dead-man's-switch pattern: wrap the job so each run is recorded with an expected cadence, then run `check`, which flags jobs that are overdue (never ran) distinctly from jobs that crashed. Ideally demonstrates with cron_sentinel.py.

assertions.text: Explains detecting overdue / never-ran jobs, not just crashes
Recommends wrapping the job and running check on a schedule
Distinguishes a silent/overdue failure from a loud crash
